revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''征新羅大将軍'''（せいしらぎたいしょうぐん）は、かつて[[日本]]で用いられた[[将軍]]の称号の一つで、[[新羅]]を...'
revision.text:
'''征新羅大将軍'''（せいしらぎたいしょうぐん）は、かつて[[日本]]で用いられた[[将軍]]の称号の一つで、[[新羅]]を[[征伐|征]]する将軍の意である。
== 概要 == 初見は[[推古]]8年（西暦[[600年]]）2月で、[[新羅]]に滅ぼされた[[任那日本府]]を救援するために新羅へ出兵した。このとき[[蘇我氏]]の一族である[[境部摩理勢]]（境部臣）が征討大将軍に任命され、副将軍は[[穂積祖足]]（穂積臣）であった。結果、五つの城を攻略し新羅を降伏させ、さらに多多羅（たたら）、素奈羅（すなら）、弗知鬼（ほちくい）、委陀（わだ）、南迦羅（ありひしのから）、阿羅々（あらら）の6つの城を攻略して、[[倭国]]（日本）への[[朝貢]]を約させた。しかし倭国の軍が帰国すると新羅は再び任那に攻め込んだ。
推古10年（[[602年]]）2月、[[聖徳太子]]の弟[[来目皇子]]が征討将軍として軍2万5千を授けられ、4月に軍を率いて[[筑紫国]]に至り、島郡に屯営した。しかし、来目皇子が病を得て新羅への進軍を延期とし、そのまま来目皇子は征討を果たせぬまま推古11年（[[603年]]）2月4日、筑紫にて薨去し計画は頓挫した。
推古11年（[[603年]]）4月、来目皇子の異母兄[[当麻皇子]]が征討将軍に任命される。同年7月3日、難波より出航し、7月6日に播磨に到着するが、妻の舎人皇女が明石に薨去したため、当麻皇子は朝廷に帰還し、計画は潰えた。
== 任官者 == * [[境部摩理勢]]（[[大将軍]]） * [[穂積祖足]]（[[副将軍]]） * [[来目皇子]]（[[将軍]]） * [[当麻皇子]]（[[将軍]]）
== 参考文献 == * 『[[日本書紀]]』推古天皇8年条。
== 関連項目 == *[[新羅征討計画]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:せいしらきたいしようくん}} [[Category:令外官]] [[Category:飛鳥時代]] [[Category:日本の将軍]]

'''征新羅大将軍'''（せいしらぎたいしょうぐん）は、かつて[[日本]]で用いられた[[将軍]]の称号の一つで、[[新羅]]を[[征伐|征]]する将軍の意である。
== 概要 == 初見は[[推古]]8年（西暦[[600年]]）2月で、[[新羅]]に滅ぼされた[[任那日本府]]を救援するために新羅へ出兵した。このとき[[蘇我氏]]の一族である[[境部摩理勢]]（境部臣）が征討大将軍に任命され、副将軍は[[穂積祖足]]（穂積臣）であった。結果、五つの城を攻略し新羅を降伏させ、さらに多多羅（たたら）、素奈羅（すなら）、弗知鬼（ほちくい）、委陀（わだ）、南迦羅（ありひしのから）、阿羅々（あらら）の6つの城を攻略して、[[倭国]]（日本）への[[朝貢]]を約させた。しかし倭国の軍が帰国すると新羅は再び任那に攻め込んだ。
推古10年（[[602年]]）2月、[[聖徳太子]]の弟[[来目皇子]]が征討将軍として軍2万5千を授けられ、4月に軍を率いて[[筑紫国]]に至り、島郡に屯営した。しかし、来目皇子が病を得て新羅への進軍を延期とし、そのまま来目皇子は征討を果たせぬまま推古11年（[[603年]]）2月4日、筑紫にて薨去し計画は頓挫した。
推古11年（[[603年]]）4月、来目皇子の異母兄[[当麻皇子]]が征討将軍に任命される。同年7月3日、難波より出航し、7月6日に播磨に到着するが、妻の舎人皇女が明石に薨去したため、当麻皇子は朝廷に帰還し、計画は潰えた。
== 任官者 == * [[境部摩理勢]]（[[大将軍]]、実際に新羅へ赴任せず） * [[穂積祖足]]（[[副将軍]]） * [[来目皇子]]（[[将軍]]、実際に新羅へ赴任せず） * [[当麻皇子]]（[[将軍]]、実際に新羅へ赴任せず）
== 参考文献 == * 『[[日本書紀]]』推古天皇8年条。
== 関連項目 == *[[新羅征討計画]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:せいしらきたいしようくん}} [[Category:令外官]] [[Category:飛鳥時代]] [[Category:日本の将軍]]

{{DEFAULTSORT:せいしらきたいしようくん}} [[Category:令外官]] [[Category:飛鳥時代]] [[Category:日本の将軍]]

'''征新羅大将軍'''（せいしらぎたいしょうぐん）は、かつて[[日本]]で用いられた[[将軍]]の称号の一つで、[[新羅]]を[[征伐|征]]する将軍の意である。[[新羅]]に滅ぼされた[[任那日本府]]を救援する目的で任命された。
== 概要 == 初見は[[推古]]8年（西暦[[600年]]）2月で、[[新羅]]に滅ぼされた[[任那日本府]]を救援するために新羅へ派遣された。このとき[[蘇我氏]]の一族である[[境部摩理勢]]（境部臣）が征討大将軍に、[[穂積祖足]]（穂積臣）が副将軍に任じられ、約1万の軍勢で新羅に出征した。[[境部摩理勢]]は名目上で、実際に出征はしていない。結果、五つの城を攻め落とし新羅を降伏させ、さらに多多羅（たたら）、素奈羅（すなら）、弗知鬼（ほちくい）、委陀（わだ）、南迦羅（ありひしのから）、阿羅々（あらら）の6つの城を攻略して、[[倭国]]（日本）への[[朝貢]]を約させた。しかし倭国の軍が帰国すると新羅は再び任那に攻め込んだ。
推古10年（[[602年]]）2月、[[聖徳太子]]の弟[[来目皇子]]が征討将軍として軍2万5千を授けられ、4月に軍を率いて[[筑紫国]]に至り、島郡に屯営した。しかし、来目皇子が病を得て新羅への進軍を延期とし、そのまま来目皇子は征討を果たせぬまま推古11年（[[603年]]）2月4日、筑紫にて薨去し計画は頓挫した。
推古11年（[[603年]]）4月、来目皇子の異母兄[[当麻皇子]]が征討将軍に任命される。同年7月3日、難波より出航し、7月6日に播磨に到着するが、妻の舎人皇女が明石に薨去したため、当麻皇子は朝廷に帰還し、計画は潰えた。
推古天皇31年（[[623]]）、中臣國が新羅が任那を討ったため新羅を討ち任那を百済につけるべきだと天皇にのべた。そこで境部雄摩侶と中臣國が大将軍に、河辺禰受、物部依網乙等、波多廣庭、近江脚身飯蓋、平群宇志、大伴闕、大宅軍らが副将軍に任じられ、数万の軍をひきいて朝鮮半島に渡った。すると軍の多さを聞いた新羅は降伏した。
== 任官者 == === 大将軍・将軍 === * [[境部摩理勢]]（実際に新羅へ赴任せず） * [[来目皇子]]（[[将軍]]、実際に新羅へ赴任せず） * [[当麻皇子]]（[[将軍]]、実際に新羅へ赴任せず） * [[中臣國]] * [[境部雄摩侶]]
=== 副将軍 === * [[穂積祖足]] * [[河辺禰受]] * [[物部依網乙等]] * [[波多廣庭]] * [[近江脚身飯蓋]] * [[平群宇志]] * [[大伴闕]] * [[大宅軍]]
== 参考文献 == * 『[[日本書紀]]』推古天皇8年条。
== 関連項目 == *[[新羅征討計画]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:せいしらきたいしようくん}} [[Category:令外官]] [[Category:飛鳥時代]] [[Category:日本の将軍]]

推古11年（[[603年]]）4月、来目皇子の異母兄[[当麻皇子]]が征討将軍に任命される。同年7月3日、難波より出航し、7月6日に播磨に到着するが、妻の舎人皇女が明石に薨去したため、当麻皇子は朝廷に帰還し、計画は潰えた。
推古天皇31年（[[623]]）、中臣國が新羅が任那を討ったため新羅を討ち任那を百済につけるべきだと天皇にのべた。そこで境部雄摩侶と中臣國が大将軍に、河辺禰受、物部依網乙等、波多廣庭、近江脚身飯蓋、平群宇志、大伴闕、大宅軍らが副将軍に任じられ、数万の軍をひきいて朝鮮半島に渡った。すると軍の多さを聞いた新羅は降伏した。
== 任官者 == === 大将軍・将軍 === * [[境部摩理勢]]（実際に新羅へ赴任せず） * [[来目皇子]]（[[将軍]]、実際に新羅へ赴任せず） * [[当麻皇子]]（[[将軍]]、実際に新羅へ赴任せず） * [[中臣國]] * [[境部雄摩侶]]
=== 副将軍 === * [[穂積祖足]] * [[河辺禰受]] * [[物部依網乙等]] * [[波多廣庭]] * [[近江脚身飯蓋]] * [[平群宇志]] * [[大伴闕]] * [[大宅軍]]
== 参考文献 == * 『[[日本書紀]]』
== 関連項目 == *[[新羅征討計画]]

'''征新羅大将軍'''（せいしらぎたいしょうぐん、しらきをうついくさのきみ）は、かつて[[日本]]で用いられた[[将軍]]の称号の一つで、[[新羅]]を[[征伐|征]]する将軍の意である。[[新羅]]に滅ぼされた[[任那日本府]]を救援する目的で任命された[[外国]]出征の大将軍。
== 概要 == 初見は[[推古]]8年（西暦[[600年]]）2月で、[[新羅]]に滅ぼされた[[任那日本府]]を救援するために新羅へ派遣された。このとき[[蘇我氏]]の一族である[[境部摩理勢]]（境部臣）が征討大将軍に、[[穂積祖足]]（穂積臣）が副将軍に任じられ、約1万の軍勢で新羅に出征した。[[境部摩理勢]]は名目上で、実際に出征はしていない。結果、五つの城を攻め落とし新羅を降伏させ、さらに多多羅（たたら）、素奈羅（すなら）、弗知鬼（ほちくい）、委陀（わだ）、南迦羅（ありひしのから）、阿羅々（あらら）の6つの城を攻略して、[[倭国]]（日本）への[[朝貢]]を約させた。しかし倭国の軍が帰国すると新羅は再び任那に攻め込んだ。
推古10年（[[602年]]）2月、[[聖徳太子]]の弟[[来目皇子]]が征討将軍として軍2万5千を授けられ、4月に軍を率いて[[筑紫国]]に至り、島郡に屯営した。しかし、来目皇子が病を得て新羅への進軍を延期とし、そのまま来目皇子は征討を果たせぬまま推古11年（[[603年]]）2月4日、筑紫にて薨去し計画は頓挫した。
推古11年（[[603年]]）4月、来目皇子の異母兄[[当麻皇子]]が征討将軍に任命される。同年7月3日、難波より出航し、7月6日に播磨に到着するが、妻の舎人皇女が明石に薨去したため、当麻皇子は朝廷に帰還し、計画は潰えた。
また、前述の三度以外にも、推古31年（[[623年]]）にも新羅征討があったとされる。これは中臣國が新羅が任那を討ったため新羅を討ち任那を百済につけるべきだと天皇にのべたことに始まる。そこで境部雄摩侶と中臣國が大将軍に、河辺禰受、物部依網乙等、波多廣庭、近江脚身飯蓋、平群宇志、大伴某（名は不詳）、大宅軍らが副将軍に任じられ、数万の軍をひきいて朝鮮半島に渡った。すると軍の多さを聞いた新羅は降伏した。
== 任官者 == === 大将軍・将軍 === * [[境部摩理勢]]（実際に新羅へ赴任せず） * [[来目皇子]]（[[将軍]]、実際に新羅へ赴任せず） * [[当麻皇子]]（[[将軍]]、実際に新羅へ赴任せず） * [[中臣國]] * [[境部雄摩侶]]
=== 副将軍 === * [[穂積祖足]] * [[河辺禰受]] * [[物部依網乙等]] * [[波多廣庭]] * [[近江脚身飯蓋]] * [[平群宇志]] * [[大伴某]]（名は不詳） * [[大宅軍]]
== 参考文献 == * 『[[日本書紀]]』
== 関連項目 == *[[新羅征討計画]]
{{Japanese-history-stub|せいしらきたいしょうくん}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:せいしらきたいしようくん}} [[Category:令外官]] [[Category:飛鳥時代]] [[Category:日本の将軍]]

== 概要 == 初見は[[推古天皇|推古]]8年（西暦[[600年]]）2月で、[[新羅]]に滅ぼされた[[任那日本府]]を救援するために新羅へ派遣された。このとき[[蘇我氏]]の一族である[[境部摩理勢]]（境部臣）が征討大将軍に、[[穂積祖足]]（穂積臣）が副将軍に任じられ、約1万の軍勢で新羅に出征した。[[境部摩理勢]]は名目上で、実際に出征はしていない。結果、5つの城を攻め落とし新羅を降伏させ、さらに多多羅（たたら）、素奈羅（すなら）、弗知鬼（ほちくい）、委陀（わだ）、南迦羅（ありひしのから）、阿羅々（あらら）の6つの城を攻略して、[[倭国]]（日本）への[[朝貢]]を約させた。しかし倭国の軍が帰国すると新羅は再び任那に攻め込んだ。
推古10年（[[602年]]）2月、[[聖徳太子]]の弟[[来目皇子]]が征討将軍として軍2万5千を授けられ、4月に軍を率いて[[筑紫国]]に至り、島郡に屯営した。しかし、来目皇子が病を得て新羅への進軍を延期とし、そのまま来目皇子は征討を果たせぬまま推古11年（[[603年]]）2月4日、筑紫にて薨去し計画は頓挫した。
推古11年（[[603年]]）4月、来目皇子の異母兄[[当麻皇子]]が征討将軍に任命される。同年7月3日、難波より出航し、7月6日に播磨に到着するが、妻の舎人皇女が明石に薨去したため、当麻皇子は朝廷に帰還し、計画は潰えた。
また、上述の3度以外にも、推古31年（[[623年]]）にも新羅征討があったとされる。これは中臣國が、新羅が任那を討ったため新羅を討ち、任那を百済につけるべきだと天皇に述べたことに始まる。そこで境部雄摩侶と中臣國が大将軍に、河辺禰受、物部依網乙等、波多廣庭、近江脚身飯蓋、平群宇志、大伴某（名は不詳）、大宅軍らが副将軍に任じられ、数万の軍をひきいて朝鮮半島に渡った。すると軍の多さを聞いた新羅は降伏した。
== 任官者 == === 大将軍・将軍 === * [[境部摩理勢]]（実際に新羅へ赴任せず） * [[来目皇子]]（[[将軍]]、実際に新羅へ赴任せず） * [[当麻皇子]]（[[将軍]]、実際に新羅へ赴任せず） * [[中臣国子|中臣國]] * [[境部雄摩侶]]
=== 副将軍 === * [[穂積祖足]] * [[河辺禰受]] * [[物部依網乙等]] * [[波多廣庭]] * [[近江脚身飯蓋]] * [[平群宇志]] * [[大伴某]]（名は不詳） * [[大宅軍]]
== 参考文献 == * 『[[日本書紀]]』
== 関連項目 == *[[新羅征討計画]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:せいしらきたいしようくん}} [[Category:令外官]] [[Category:飛鳥時代]] [[Category:日本の将軍の称号]] [[Category:古代日朝関係]] [[Category:新羅]]

revision.text:
'''征新羅大将軍'''（せいしらぎたいしょうぐん、しらきをうついくさのきみ）は、かつて[[日本]]で用いられた[[将軍]]の称号の一つで、[[新羅]]を[[征伐|征]]する将軍の意である。[[新羅]]に滅ぼされた[[任那日本府]]を救援する目的で任命された外国出征の大将軍。
== 概要 == 初見は[[推古天皇|推古]]8年（西暦[[600年]]）2月で、[[新羅]]に滅ぼされた[[任那日本府]]を救援するために新羅へ派遣された。このとき[[蘇我氏]]の一族である[[境部摩理勢]]（境部臣）が征討大将軍に、[[穂積祖足]]（穂積臣）が副将軍に任じられ、約1万の軍勢で新羅に出征した。[[境部摩理勢]]は名目上で、実際に出征はしていない。結果、5つの城を攻め落とし新羅を降伏させ、さらに多多羅（たたら）、素奈羅（すなら）、弗知鬼（ほちくい）、委陀（わだ）、南迦羅（ありひしのから）、阿羅々（あらら）の6つの城を攻略して、[[倭国]]（日本）への[[朝貢]]を約させた。しかし倭国の軍が帰国すると新羅は再び任那に攻め込んだ。
推古10年（[[602年]]）2月、[[聖徳太子]]の弟[[来目皇子]]が征討将軍として軍2万5千を授けられ、4月に軍を率いて[[筑紫国]]に至り、島郡に屯営した。しかし、来目皇子が病を得て新羅への進軍を延期とし、そのまま来目皇子は征討を果たせぬまま推古11年（[[603年]]）2月4日、筑紫にて薨去し計画は頓挫した。
推古11年（[[603年]]）4月、来目皇子の異母兄[[当麻皇子]]が征討将軍に任命される。同年7月3日、難波より出航し、7月6日に播磨に到着するが、妻の舎人皇女が明石に薨去したため、当麻皇子は朝廷に帰還し、計画は潰えた。
また、上述の3度以外にも、推古31年（[[623年]]）にも新羅征討があったとされる。これは中臣國が、新羅が任那を討ったため新羅を討ち、任那を百済につけるべきだと天皇に述べたことに始まる。そこで境部雄摩侶と中臣國が大将軍に、河辺禰受、物部依網乙等、波多廣庭、近江脚身飯蓋、平群宇志、大伴某（名は不詳）、大宅軍らが副将軍に任じられ、数万の軍をひきいて朝鮮半島に渡った。すると軍の多さを聞いた新羅は降伏した。
== 任官者 == === 大将軍・将軍 === * [[境部摩理勢]]（実際に新羅へ赴任せず） * [[来目皇子]]（[[将軍]]、実際に新羅へ赴任せず） * [[当麻皇子]]（[[将軍]]、実際に新羅へ赴任せず） * [[中臣国子|中臣國]] * [[境部雄摩侶]]
=== 副将軍 === * [[穂積祖足]] * [[河辺禰受]] * [[物部依網乙等]] * [[波多廣庭]] * [[近江脚身飯蓋]] * [[平群宇志]] * [[大伴某]]（名は不詳） * [[大宅軍]]
== 参考文献 == * 『[[日本書紀]]』
== 関連項目 == *[[新羅征討計画]]

revision.text:
'''Sei Shiraiki Taishōgun''' (征新羅大将軍, "General for the Conquest of Silla") was a title used in ancient Japan for generals appointed to lead military expeditions against the kingdom of {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}. The role was primarily created to reclaim the {{ill|Mimana Nihonfu|ja|任那日本府}}, which had been overtaken by Silla.
== Overview == The title first appears in {{ill|Suiko 8|ja|推古天皇8th year }} (600 AD). It was established during an expedition to rescue Mimana, which had been annexed by Silla. {{ill|Sakaibe no Marise|ja|境部摩理勢}}, a member of the Soga clan, was appointed as the commander-in-chief (Sei Shiraiki Taishōgun), and {{ill|Hozumi no Suketari|ja|穂積祖足}} was appointed as the deputy general (fuku-shōgun). They led an army of approximately 10,000 troops to the Korean Peninsula. Although Sakaibe no Marise held the title, he did not personally take part in the campaign. The campaign was initially successful, capturing five fortresses and forcing Silla's surrender. They further captured six additional fortresses—Tatara, Sunara, Hochikui, Wada, Arishi no Kara, and Arara—and secured a promise of tribute to Japan. However, once the Japanese forces withdrew, Silla resumed its aggression against Mimana.
In Suiko 10 (602), {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子}}, a younger brother of {{ill|Shōtoku Taishi|ja|聖徳太子}}, was appointed as the expedition general (shōgun) and given command of 25,000 troops. He advanced to {{ill|Tsukushi Province|ja|筑紫国}} in April and established a camp in the Shimogōri region. However, Prince Kume fell ill and delayed the campaign. He died in February of the following year (603), leaving the mission unfulfilled.
In Suiko 11 (603), {{ill|Prince Tōma|ja|当麻皇子}}, an older half-brother of Prince Kume, was appointed as the expedition general. He departed from Naniwa on July 3 and arrived in Harima on July 6. However, the campaign was abandoned when his wife, Princess Toneri, passed away in Akashi. Prince Tōma returned to the court, and the campaign was suspended.
Another expedition occurred in Suiko 31 (623). {{ill|Nakatomi no Kuni|ja|中臣国}} proposed to the Emperor that Japan should retaliate against Silla for attacking Mimana and restore Mimana to Baekje. As a result, {{ill|Sakaibe no Umamaro|ja|境部雄摩侶}} and Nakatomi no Kuni were appointed as generals, with several other notable figures, including {{ill|Kawabe no Neju|ja|河辺禰受}}, {{ill|Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|ja|物部依網乙等}}, and {{ill|Hada no Hiroba|ja|波多廣庭}}, serving as deputy generals. They led an army of tens of thousands to the Korean Peninsula, and upon hearing of the army’s size, Silla surrendered.
== Appointments == === Generals === * {{ill|Sakaibe no Marise|ja|境部摩理勢}} (did not participate in the campaign) * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子}} (did not complete the campaign) * {{ill|Prince Tōma|ja|当麻皇子}} (did not complete the campaign) * {{ill|Nakatomi no Kuni|ja|中臣国}} * {{ill|Sakaibe no Umamaro|ja|境部雄摩侶}}
=== Deputy Generals === * {{ill|Hozumi no Suketari|ja|穂積祖足}} * {{ill|Kawabe no Neju|ja|河辺禰受}} * {{ill|Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|ja|物部依網乙等}} * {{ill|Hada no Hiroba|ja|波多廣庭}} * {{ill|Ōmi no Ashikami no Iifuta|ja|近江脚身飯蓋}} * {{ill|Heguri no Ushi|ja|平群宇志}} * {{ill|Ōtomo no (unknown name)|ja|大伴某}} * {{ill|Ōyake no Ikusa|ja|大宅軍}}
== References == * {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} == See Also == * {{ill|Plans for the Conquest of Silla|ja|新羅征討計画}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

They led an army of tens of thousands to the Korean Peninsula, and upon hearing of the army’s size, Silla surrendered.

revision.text:
'''Sei Shiraiki Taishōgun''' (征新羅大将軍, "General for the Conquest of Silla") was a title used in ancient Japan for generals appointed to lead military expeditions against the kingdom of {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅|12=draft}}. The role was primarily created to reclaim the {{ill|Mimana Nihonfu|ja|任那日本府|12=draft}}, which had been overtaken by Silla.
== Overview == The title first appears in {{ill|Suiko 8|ja|推古天皇8th year|12=draft}} (600 AD). It was established during an expedition to rescue Mimana, which had been annexed by Silla. {{ill|Sakaibe no Marise|ja|境部摩理勢|12=draft}}, a member of the Soga clan, was appointed as the commander-in-chief (Sei Shiraiki Taishōgun), and {{ill|Hozumi no Suketari|ja|穂積祖足|12=draft}} was appointed as the deputy general (fuku-shōgun). They led an army of approximately 10,000 troops to the Korean Peninsula. Although Sakaibe no Marise held the title, he did not personally take part in the campaign. The campaign was initially successful, capturing five fortresses and forcing Silla's surrender. They further captured six additional fortresses—Tatara, Sunara, Hochikui, Wada, Arishi no Kara, and Arara—and secured a promise of tribute to Japan. However, once the Japanese forces withdrew, Silla resumed its aggression against Mimana.
In Suiko 10 (602), {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|12=draft}}, a younger brother of {{ill|Shōtoku Taishi|ja|聖徳太子|12=draft}}, was appointed as the expedition general (shōgun) and given command of 25,000 troops. He advanced to {{ill|Tsukushi Province|ja|筑紫国|12=draft}} in April and established a camp in the Shimogōri region. However, Prince Kume fell ill and delayed the campaign. He died in February of the following year (603), leaving the mission unfulfilled.
In Suiko 11 (603), {{ill|Prince Tōma|ja|当麻皇子|12=draft}}, an older half-brother of Prince Kume, was appointed as the expedition general. He departed from Naniwa on July 3 and arrived in Harima on July 6. However, the campaign was abandoned when his wife, Princess Toneri, passed away in Akashi. Prince Tōma returned to the court, and the campaign was suspended.
Another expedition occurred in Suiko 31 (623). {{ill|Nakatomi no Kuni|ja|中臣国|12=draft}} proposed to the Emperor that Japan should retaliate against Silla for attacking Mimana and restore Mimana to Baekje. As a result, {{ill|Sakaibe no Umamaro|ja|境部雄摩侶|12=draft}} and Nakatomi no Kuni were appointed as generals, with several other notable figures, including {{ill|Kawabe no Neju|ja|河辺禰受|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|ja|物部依網乙等|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Hada no Hiroba|ja|波多廣庭|12=draft}}, serving as deputy generals. They led an army of tens of thousands to the Korean Peninsula, and upon hearing of the army’s size, Silla surrendered.

== Appointments == === Generals === * {{ill|Sakaibe no Marise|ja|境部摩理勢|12=draft}} (did not participate in the campaign) * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|12=draft}} (did not complete the campaign) * {{ill|Prince Tōma|ja|当麻皇子|12=draft}} (did not complete the campaign) * {{ill|Nakatomi no Kuni|ja|中臣国|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sakaibe no Umamaro|ja|境部雄摩侶|12=draft}}
=== Deputy Generals === * {{ill|Hozumi no Suketari|ja|穂積祖足|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kawabe no Neju|ja|河辺禰受|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|ja|物部依網乙等|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hada no Hiroba|ja|波多廣庭|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōmi no Ashikami no Iifuta|ja|近江脚身飯蓋|12=draft}} * {{ill|Heguri no Ushi|ja|平群宇志|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōtomo no (unknown name)|ja|大伴某|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōyake no Ikusa|ja|大宅軍|12=draft}}

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Sei Shiraiki Taishōgun''' (征新羅大将軍, "General for the Conquest of Silla") was a title used in ancient Japan for generals appointed to lead military expeditions against the kingdom of {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅|12=draft}}. The role was primarily created to reclaim the {{ill|Mimana Nihonfu|ja|任那日本府|12=draft}}, which had been overtaken by Silla.
== Overview == The title first appears in {{ill|Suiko 8|ja|推古天皇8th year|12=draft}} (600 AD). It was established during an expedition to rescue Mimana, which had been annexed by Silla. {{ill|Sakaibe no Marise|ja|境部摩理勢|12=draft}}, a member of the Soga clan, was appointed as the commander-in-chief (Sei Shiraiki Taishōgun), and {{ill|Hozumi no Suketari|ja|穂積祖足|12=draft}} was appointed as the deputy general (fuku-shōgun). They led an army of approximately 10,000 troops to the Korean Peninsula. Although Sakaibe no Marise held the title, he did not personally take part in the campaign. The campaign was initially successful, capturing five fortresses and forcing Silla's surrender. They further captured six additional fortresses—Tatara, Sunara, Hochikui, Wada, Arishi no Kara, and Arara—and secured a promise of tribute to Japan. However, once the Japanese forces withdrew, Silla resumed its aggression against Mimana.
In Suiko 10 (602), {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|12=draft}}, a younger brother of {{ill|Shōtoku Taishi|ja|聖徳太子|12=draft}}, was appointed as the expedition general (shōgun) and given command of 25,000 troops. He advanced to {{ill|Tsukushi Province|ja|筑紫国|12=draft}} in April and established a camp in the Shimogōri region. However, Prince Kume fell ill and delayed the campaign. He died in February of the following year (603), leaving the mission unfulfilled.
In Suiko 11 (603), {{ill|Prince Tōma|ja|当麻皇子|12=draft}}, an older half-brother of Prince Kume, was appointed as the expedition general. He departed from Naniwa on July 3 and arrived in Harima on July 6. However, the campaign was abandoned when his wife, Princess Toneri, passed away in Akashi. Prince Tōma returned to the court, and the campaign was suspended.
Another expedition occurred in Suiko 31 (623). {{ill|Nakatomi no Kuni|ja|中臣国|12=draft}} proposed to the Emperor that Japan should retaliate against Silla for attacking Mimana and restore Mimana to Baekje. As a result, {{ill|Sakaibe no Umamaro|ja|境部雄摩侶|12=draft}} and Nakatomi no Kuni were appointed as generals, with several other notable figures, including {{ill|Kawabe no Neju|ja|河辺禰受|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|ja|物部依網乙等|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Hada no Hiroba|ja|波多廣庭|12=draft}}, serving as deputy generals. They led an army of tens of thousands to the Korean Peninsula, and upon hearing of the army’s size, Silla surrendered.
== Appointments == === Generals === * {{ill|Sakaibe no Marise|ja|境部摩理勢|12=draft}} (did not participate in the campaign) * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|12=draft}} (did not complete the campaign) * {{ill|Prince Tōma|ja|当麻皇子|12=draft}} (did not complete the campaign) * {{ill|Nakatomi no Kuni|ja|中臣国|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sakaibe no Umamaro|ja|境部雄摩侶|12=draft}}
=== Deputy Generals === * {{ill|Hozumi no Suketari|ja|穂積祖足|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kawabe no Neju|ja|河辺禰受|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|ja|物部依網乙等|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hada no Hiroba|ja|波多廣庭|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōmi no Ashikami no Iifuta|ja|近江脚身飯蓋|12=draft}} * {{ill|Heguri no Ushi|ja|平群宇志|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōtomo no (unknown name)|ja|大伴某|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōyake no Ikusa|ja|大宅軍|12=draft}}
== References == * {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}} == See Also == * {{ill|Plans for the Conquest of Silla|ja|新羅征討計画|12=draft}}

revision.text:
'''征新羅大将軍'''（せいしらぎたいしょうぐん、しらきをうついくさのきみ）は、かつて[[日本]]で用いられた[[将軍]]の称号の一つで、[[新羅]]を[[征伐|征]]する将軍の意である。[[新羅]]に滅ぼされた[[任那日本府]]を救援する目的で任命された外国出征の大将軍。
== 概要 == 初見は[[推古天皇|推古]]8年（西暦[[600年]]）2月で、[[新羅]]に滅ぼされた[[任那日本府]]を救援するために新羅へ派遣された。このとき[[蘇我氏]]の一族である[[境部摩理勢]]（境部臣）が征討大将軍に、[[穂積祖足]]（穂積臣）が副将軍に任じられ、約1万の軍勢で新羅に出征した。[[境部摩理勢]]は名目上で、実際に出征はしていない。結果、5つの城を攻め落とし新羅を降伏させ、さらに多多羅（たたら）、素奈羅（すなら）、弗知鬼（ほちくい）、委陀（わだ）、南迦羅（ありひしのから）、阿羅々（あらら）の6つの城を攻略して、[[倭国]]（日本）への[[朝貢]]を約させた。しかし倭国の軍が帰国すると新羅は再び任那に攻め込んだ。
推古10年（[[602年]]）2月、[[聖徳太子]]の弟[[来目皇子]]が征討将軍として軍2万5千を授けられ、4月に軍を率いて[[筑紫国]]に至り、島郡に屯営した。しかし、来目皇子が病を得て新羅への進軍を延期とし、そのまま来目皇子は征討を果たせぬまま推古11年（[[603年]]）2月4日、筑紫にて薨去し計画は頓挫した。
推古11年（[[603年]]）4月、来目皇子の異母兄[[当麻皇子]]が征討将軍に任命される。同年7月3日、難波より出航し、7月6日に播磨に到着するが、妻の舎人皇女が明石に薨去したため、当麻皇子は朝廷に帰還し、計画は潰えた。
また、上述の3度以外にも、推古31年（[[623年]]）にも新羅征討があったとされる。これは中臣國が、新羅が任那を討ったため新羅を討ち、任那を百済につけるべきだと天皇に述べたことに始まる。そこで境部雄摩侶と中臣國が大将軍に、河辺禰受、物部依網乙等、波多廣庭、近江脚身飯蓋、平群宇志、大伴某（名は不詳）、大宅軍らが副将軍に任じられ、数万の軍をひきいて朝鮮半島に渡った。すると軍の多さを聞いた新羅は降伏した。
== 任官者 == === 大将軍・将軍 === * [[境部摩理勢]]（実際に新羅へ赴任せず） * [[来目皇子]]（[[将軍]]、実際に新羅へ赴任せず） * [[当麻皇子]]（[[将軍]]、実際に新羅へ赴任せず） * [[中臣国子|中臣國]] * [[境部雄摩侶]]
=== 副将軍 === * [[穂積祖足]] * [[河辺禰受]] * [[物部依網乙等]] * [[波多廣庭]] * [[近江脚身飯蓋]] * [[平群宇志]] * [[大伴某]]（名は不詳） * [[大宅軍]]
== 参考文献 == * 『[[日本書紀]]』
== 関連項目 == *[[新羅征討計画]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:せいしらきたいしようくん}} [[Category:令外官]] [[Category:飛鳥時代]] [[Category:日本の将軍の称号]] [[Category:古代日朝関係]] [[Category:新羅の国際関係]]

=== 副将軍 === * [[穂積祖足]] * [[河辺禰受]] * [[物部依網乙等]] * [[波多廣庭]] * [[近江脚身飯蓋]] * [[平群宇志]] * [[大伴某]]（名は不詳） * [[大宅軍]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:征新羅大将軍]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q20040917]]
Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Sei Shiraiki Taishōgun''' (征新羅大将軍, "General for the Conquest of Silla") was a title used in ancient Japan for generals appointed to lead military expeditions against the kingdom of [[:en:Silla|Silla]]. The role was primarily created to reclaim the {{ill|Mimana Nihonfu|ja|任那日本府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimana Nihonfu|qq=|1=Mimana Nihon-fu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6139814}}, which had been overtaken by Silla.

== Overview ==
The title first appears in {{ill|Suiko 8|ja|推古天皇8年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suiko 8|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (600 AD). It was established during an expedition to rescue Mimana, which had been annexed by Silla. [[:en:Sakaibe no Marise|Sakaibe no Marise]], a member of the Soga clan, was appointed as the commander-in-chief (Sei Shiraiki Taishōgun), and {{ill|Hozumi no Suketari|ja|穂積祖足|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi no Suketari|qq=|1=Hozumi Soso|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597084}} was appointed as the deputy general (fuku-shōgun). They led an army of approximately 10,000 troops to the Korean Peninsula. Although Sakaibe no Marise held the title, he did not personally take part in the campaign. The campaign was initially successful, capturing five fortresses and forcing Silla's surrender. They further captured six additional fortresses—Tatara, Sunara, Hochikui, Wada, Arishi no Kara, and Arara—and secured a promise of tribute to Japan. However, once the Japanese forces withdrew, Silla resumed its aggression against Mimana.

In Suiko 10 (602), {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, a younger brother of [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Shōtoku Taishi]], was appointed as the expedition general (shōgun) and given command of 25,000 troops. He advanced to [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Tsukushi Province]] in April and established a camp in the Shimogōri region. However, Prince Kume fell ill and delayed the campaign. He died in February of the following year (603), leaving the mission unfulfilled.

In Suiko 11 (603), {{ill|Prince Maroko|lt=Prince Maroko|lt=Prince Tōma|ja|当麻皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tōma|qq=}}, an older half-brother of Prince Kume, was appointed as the expedition general. He departed from Naniwa on July 3 and arrived in Harima on July 6. However, the campaign was abandoned when his wife, Princess Toneri, passed away in Akashi. Prince Tōma returned to the court, and the campaign was suspended.

Another expedition occurred in Suiko 31 (623). {{ill|Nakatomi no Kuni|ja|中臣国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kuni|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣国子|1=Nakatomi Kuniko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407186}} proposed to the Emperor that Japan should retaliate against Silla for attacking Mimana and restore Mimana to Baekje. As a result, {{ill|Sakaibe no Umamaro|ja|境部雄摩侶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaibe no Umamaro|qq=|1=Sakaibe no Umamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407177}} and Nakatomi no Kuni were appointed as generals, with several other notable figures, including {{ill|Kawabe no Neju|ja|河辺禰受|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe no Neju|qq=|1=Kawabe no Nezu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407260}}, {{ill|Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|ja|物部依網乙等|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|qq=|1=Mononobe no Yosami no Oto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66365099}}, and {{ill|Hada no Hiroba|ja|波多廣庭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hada no Hiroba|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, serving as deputy generals. They led an army of tens of thousands to the Korean Peninsula, and upon hearing of the army’s size, Silla surrendered.

== Appointments ==
=== Generals ===
* [[:en:Sakaibe no Marise|Sakaibe no Marise]] (did not participate in the campaign)
* {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}} (did not complete the campaign)
* {{ill|Prince Maroko|lt=Prince Maroko|lt=Prince Tōma|ja|当麻皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tōma|qq=}} (did not complete the campaign)
* {{ill|Nakatomi no Kuni|ja|中臣国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kuni|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣国子|1=Nakatomi Kuniko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407186}}
* {{ill|Sakaibe no Umamaro|ja|境部雄摩侶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaibe no Umamaro|qq=|1=Sakaibe no Umamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407177}}

=== Deputy Generals ===
* {{ill|Hozumi no Suketari|ja|穂積祖足|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi no Suketari|qq=|1=Hozumi Soso|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597084}}
* {{ill|Kawabe no Neju|ja|河辺禰受|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe no Neju|qq=|1=Kawabe no Nezu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407260}}
* {{ill|Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|ja|物部依網乙等|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|qq=|1=Mononobe no Yosami no Oto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66365099}}
* {{ill|Hada no Hiroba|ja|波多廣庭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hada no Hiroba|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Ōmi no Ashikami no Iifuta|ja|近江脚身飯蓋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi no Ashikami no Iifuta|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Heguri no Ushi|ja|平群宇志|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heguri no Ushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Ōtomo no (unknown name)|ja|大伴某|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no (unknown name)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Ōyake no Ikusa|ja|大宅軍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyake no Ikusa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

== References ==
* [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Plans for the Conquest of Silla|ja|新羅征討計画|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Plans for the Conquest of Silla|qq=|1=Fujiwara no Nakamaro's planned invasion of Silla|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10307032}}

{{Japanese-history-stub}}

{{translated page|ja|征新羅大将軍|version=100686067|comment=Imported full ja history}}

As a result, {{ill|Sakaibe no Umamaro|ja|境部雄摩侶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaibe no Umamaro|qq=|1=Sakaibe no Umamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407177}} and Nakatomi no Kuni were appointed as generals, with several other notable figures, including {{ill|Kawabe no Neju|ja|河辺禰受|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe no Neju|qq=|1=Kawabe no Nezu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407260}}, {{ill|Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|ja|物部依網乙等|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|qq=|1=Mononobe no Yosami no Oto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66365099}}, and {{ill|Hada no Hiroba|ja|波多廣庭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hada no Hiroba|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, serving as deputy generals. They led an army of tens of thousands to the Korean Peninsula, and upon hearing of the army’s size, Silla surrendered.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:征新羅大将軍]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q20040917]]
Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Sei Shiraiki Taishōgun''' (征新羅大将軍, "General for the Conquest of Silla") was a title used in ancient Japan for generals appointed to lead military expeditions against the kingdom of [[:en:Silla|Silla]]. The role was primarily created to reclaim the {{ill|Mimana Nihonfu|ja|任那日本府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimana Nihonfu|1=Mimana Nihon-fu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6139814}}, which had been overtaken by Silla.

== Overview ==
The title first appears in {{ill|Suiko 8|ja|推古天皇8年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suiko 8|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (600 AD). It was established during an expedition to rescue Mimana, which had been annexed by Silla. [[:en:Sakaibe no Marise|Sakaibe no Marise]], a member of the Soga clan, was appointed as the commander-in-chief (Sei Shiraiki Taishōgun), and {{ill|Hozumi no Suketari|ja|穂積祖足|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi no Suketari|1=Hozumi Soso|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597084}} was appointed as the deputy general (fuku-shōgun). They led an army of approximately 10,000 troops to the Korean Peninsula. Although Sakaibe no Marise held the title, he did not personally take part in the campaign. The campaign was initially successful, capturing five fortresses and forcing Silla's surrender. They further captured six additional fortresses—Tatara, Sunara, Hochikui, Wada, Arishi no Kara, and Arara—and secured a promise of tribute to Japan. However, once the Japanese forces withdrew, Silla resumed its aggression against Mimana.

In Suiko 10 (602), {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, a younger brother of [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Shōtoku Taishi]], was appointed as the expedition general (shōgun) and given command of 25,000 troops. He advanced to [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Tsukushi Province]] in April and established a camp in the Shimogōri region. However, Prince Kume fell ill and delayed the campaign. He died in February of the following year (603), leaving the mission unfulfilled.

In Suiko 11 (603), {{ill|Prince Maroko|lt=Prince Maroko|lt=Prince Tōma|ja|当麻皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tōma|qq=}}, an older half-brother of Prince Kume, was appointed as the expedition general. He departed from Naniwa on July 3 and arrived in Harima on July 6. However, the campaign was abandoned when his wife, Princess Toneri, passed away in Akashi. Prince Tōma returned to the court, and the campaign was suspended.

Another expedition occurred in Suiko 31 (623). {{ill|Nakatomi no Kuni|ja|中臣国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kuni|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣国子|1=Nakatomi Kuniko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407186}} proposed to the Emperor that Japan should retaliate against Silla for attacking Mimana and restore Mimana to Baekje. As a result, {{ill|Sakaibe no Umamaro|ja|境部雄摩侶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaibe no Umamaro|1=Sakaibe no Umamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407177}} and Nakatomi no Kuni were appointed as generals, with several other notable figures, including {{ill|Kawabe no Neju|ja|河辺禰受|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe no Neju|1=Kawabe no Nezu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407260}}, {{ill|Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|ja|物部依網乙等|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|1=Mononobe no Yosami no Oto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66365099}}, and {{ill|Hada no Hiroba|ja|波多廣庭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hada no Hiroba|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, serving as deputy generals. They led an army of tens of thousands to the Korean Peninsula, and upon hearing of the army’s size, Silla surrendered.

== Appointments ==
=== Generals ===
* [[:en:Sakaibe no Marise|Sakaibe no Marise]] (did not participate in the campaign)
* {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}} (did not complete the campaign)
* {{ill|Prince Maroko|lt=Prince Maroko|lt=Prince Tōma|ja|当麻皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tōma|qq=}} (did not complete the campaign)
* {{ill|Nakatomi no Kuni|ja|中臣国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kuni|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣国子|1=Nakatomi Kuniko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407186}}
* {{ill|Sakaibe no Umamaro|ja|境部雄摩侶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaibe no Umamaro|1=Sakaibe no Umamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407177}}

=== Deputy Generals ===
* {{ill|Hozumi no Suketari|ja|穂積祖足|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi no Suketari|1=Hozumi Soso|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597084}}
* {{ill|Kawabe no Neju|ja|河辺禰受|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe no Neju|1=Kawabe no Nezu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407260}}
* {{ill|Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|ja|物部依網乙等|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|1=Mononobe no Yosami no Oto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66365099}}
* {{ill|Hada no Hiroba|ja|波多廣庭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hada no Hiroba|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Ōmi no Ashikami no Iifuta|ja|近江脚身飯蓋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi no Ashikami no Iifuta|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Heguri no Ushi|ja|平群宇志|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heguri no Ushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Ōtomo no (unknown name)|ja|大伴某|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no (unknown name)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Ōyake no Ikusa|ja|大宅軍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyake no Ikusa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Sei Shiraiki Taishōgun''' (征新羅大将軍, "General for the Conquest of Silla") was a title used in ancient Japan for generals appointed to lead military expeditions against the kingdom of [[:en:Silla|Silla]]. The role was primarily created to reclaim the {{ill|Mimana Nihonfu|ja|任那日本府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimana Nihonfu|1=Mimana Nihon-fu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6139814}}, which had been overtaken by Silla.

== Overview ==
The title first appears in {{ill|Suiko 8|ja|推古天皇8年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suiko 8|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (600 AD). It was established during an expedition to rescue Mimana, which had been annexed by Silla. [[:en:Sakaibe no Marise|Sakaibe no Marise]], a member of the Soga clan, was appointed as the commander-in-chief (Sei Shiraiki Taishōgun), and {{ill|Hozumi no Suketari|ja|穂積祖足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi no Suketari|1=Hozumi Soso|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597084}} was appointed as the deputy general (fuku-shōgun). They led an army of approximately 10,000 troops to the Korean Peninsula. Although Sakaibe no Marise held the title, he did not personally take part in the campaign. The campaign was initially successful, capturing five fortresses and forcing Silla's surrender. They further captured six additional fortresses—Tatara, Sunara, Hochikui, Wada, Arishi no Kara, and Arara—and secured a promise of tribute to Japan. However, once the Japanese forces withdrew, Silla resumed its aggression against Mimana.

In Suiko 10 (602), {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, a younger brother of [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Shōtoku Taishi]], was appointed as the expedition general (shōgun) and given command of 25,000 troops. He advanced to [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Tsukushi Province]] in April and established a camp in the Shimogōri region. However, Prince Kume fell ill and delayed the campaign. He died in February of the following year (603), leaving the mission unfulfilled.

In Suiko 11 (603), {{ill|Prince Maroko|lt=Prince Maroko|lt=Prince Tōma|ja|当麻皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tōma|qq=}}, an older half-brother of Prince Kume, was appointed as the expedition general. He departed from Naniwa on July 3 and arrived in Harima on July 6. However, the campaign was abandoned when his wife, Princess Toneri, passed away in Akashi. Prince Tōma returned to the court, and the campaign was suspended.

Another expedition occurred in Suiko 31 (623). {{ill|Nakatomi no Kuni|ja|中臣国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kuni|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣国子|1=Nakatomi Kuniko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407186}} proposed to the Emperor that Japan should retaliate against Silla for attacking Mimana and restore Mimana to Baekje. As a result, {{ill|Sakaibe no Umamaro|ja|境部雄摩侶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaibe no Umamaro|1=Sakaibe no Umamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407177}} and Nakatomi no Kuni were appointed as generals, with several other notable figures, including {{ill|Kawabe no Neju|ja|河辺禰受|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe no Neju|1=Kawabe no Nezu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407260}}, {{ill|Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|ja|物部依網乙等|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|1=Mononobe no Yosami no Oto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66365099}}, and {{ill|Hada no Hiroba|ja|波多廣庭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hada no Hiroba|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, serving as deputy generals. They led an army of tens of thousands to the Korean Peninsula, and upon hearing of the army’s size, Silla surrendered.

== Appointments ==
=== Generals ===
* [[:en:Sakaibe no Marise|Sakaibe no Marise]] (did not participate in the campaign)
* {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}} (did not complete the campaign)
* {{ill|Prince Maroko|lt=Prince Maroko|lt=Prince Tōma|ja|当麻皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tōma|qq=}} (did not complete the campaign)
* {{ill|Nakatomi no Kuni|ja|中臣国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kuni|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣国子|1=Nakatomi Kuniko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407186}}
* {{ill|Sakaibe no Umamaro|ja|境部雄摩侶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaibe no Umamaro|1=Sakaibe no Umamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407177}}

=== Deputy Generals ===
* {{ill|Hozumi no Suketari|ja|穂積祖足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi no Suketari|1=Hozumi Soso|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597084}}
* {{ill|Kawabe no Neju|ja|河辺禰受|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe no Neju|1=Kawabe no Nezu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407260}}
* {{ill|Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|ja|物部依網乙等|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|1=Mononobe no Yosami no Oto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66365099}}
* {{ill|Hada no Hiroba|ja|波多廣庭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hada no Hiroba|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Ōmi no Ashikami no Iifuta|ja|近江脚身飯蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi no Ashikami no Iifuta|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Heguri no Ushi|ja|平群宇志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heguri no Ushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Ōtomo no (unknown name)|ja|大伴某|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no (unknown name)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Ōyake no Ikusa|ja|大宅軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyake no Ikusa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

== References ==

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:征新羅大将軍]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q20040917]]
Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Sei Shiraiki Taishōgun''' (征新羅大将軍, "General for the Conquest of Silla") was a title used in ancient Japan for generals appointed to lead military expeditions against the kingdom of [[:en:Silla|Silla]]. The role was primarily created to reclaim the {{ill|Mimana Nihonfu|ja|任那日本府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimana Nihonfu|1=Mimana Nihon-fu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6139814}}, which had been overtaken by Silla.

== Overview ==
The title first appears in {{ill|Suiko 8|ja|推古天皇8年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suiko 8|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (600 AD). It was established during an expedition to rescue Mimana, which had been annexed by Silla. [[:en:Sakaibe no Marise|Sakaibe no Marise]], a member of the Soga clan, was appointed as the commander-in-chief (Sei Shiraiki Taishōgun), and {{ill|Hozumi no Suketari|ja|穂積祖足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi no Suketari|1=Hozumi Soso|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597084}} was appointed as the deputy general (fuku-shōgun). They led an army of approximately 10,000 troops to the Korean Peninsula. Although Sakaibe no Marise held the title, he did not personally take part in the campaign. The campaign was initially successful, capturing five fortresses and forcing Silla's surrender. They further captured six additional fortresses—Tatara, Sunara, Hochikui, Wada, Arishi no Kara, and Arara—and secured a promise of tribute to Japan. However, once the Japanese forces withdrew, Silla resumed its aggression against Mimana.

In Suiko 10 (602), {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, a younger brother of [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Shōtoku Taishi]], was appointed as the expedition general (shōgun) and given command of 25,000 troops. He advanced to [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Tsukushi Province]] in April and established a camp in the Shimogōri region. However, Prince Kume fell ill and delayed the campaign. He died in February of the following year (603), leaving the mission unfulfilled.

In Suiko 11 (603), {{ill|Prince Maroko|lt=Prince Maroko|lt=Prince Tōma|ja|当麻皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tōma|qq=}}, an older half-brother of Prince Kume, was appointed as the expedition general. He departed from Naniwa on July 3 and arrived in Harima on July 6. However, the campaign was abandoned when his wife, Princess Toneri, passed away in Akashi. Prince Tōma returned to the court, and the campaign was suspended.

Another expedition occurred in Suiko 31 (623). {{ill|Nakatomi no Kuni|ja|中臣国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kuni|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣国子|1=Nakatomi Kuniko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407186}} proposed to the Emperor that Japan should retaliate against Silla for attacking Mimana and restore Mimana to Baekje. As a result, {{ill|Sakaibe no Umamaro|ja|境部雄摩侶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaibe no Umamaro|1=Sakaibe no Umamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407177}} and Nakatomi no Kuni were appointed as generals, with several other notable figures, including {{ill|Kawabe no Neju|ja|河辺禰受|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe no Neju|1=Kawabe no Nezu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407260}}, {{ill|Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|ja|物部依網乙等|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|1=Mononobe no Yosami no Oto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66365099}}, and {{ill|Hada no Hiroba|ja|波多廣庭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hada no Hiroba|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, serving as deputy generals. They led an army of tens of thousands to the Korean Peninsula, and upon hearing of the army’s size, Silla surrendered.

== Appointments ==
=== Generals ===
* [[:en:Sakaibe no Marise|Sakaibe no Marise]] (did not participate in the campaign)
* {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}} (did not complete the campaign)
* {{ill|Prince Maroko|lt=Prince Maroko|lt=Prince Tōma|ja|当麻皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tōma|qq=}} (did not complete the campaign)
* {{ill|Nakatomi no Kuni|ja|中臣国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kuni|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣国子|1=Nakatomi Kuniko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407186}}
* {{ill|Sakaibe no Umamaro|ja|境部雄摩侶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaibe no Umamaro|1=Sakaibe no Umamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407177}}

=== Deputy Generals ===
* {{ill|Hozumi no Suketari|ja|穂積祖足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi no Suketari|1=Hozumi Soso|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597084}}
* {{ill|Kawabe no Neju|ja|河辺禰受|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe no Neju|1=Kawabe no Nezu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407260}}
* {{ill|Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|ja|物部依網乙等|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|1=Mononobe no Yosami no Oto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66365099}}
* {{ill|Hada no Hiroba|ja|波多廣庭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hada no Hiroba|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Ōmi no Ashikami no Iifuta|ja|近江脚身飯蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi no Ashikami no Iifuta|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Heguri no Ushi|ja|平群宇志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heguri no Ushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Ōtomo no (unknown name)|ja|大伴某|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no (unknown name)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Ōyake no Ikusa|ja|大宅軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyake no Ikusa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:征新羅大将軍]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q20040917]]
Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Sei Shiraiki Taishōgun''' (征新羅大将軍, "General for the Conquest of Silla") was a title used in ancient Japan for generals appointed to lead military expeditions against the kingdom of [[:en:Silla|Silla]]. The role was primarily created to reclaim the {{ill|Mimana Nihonfu|ja|任那日本府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimana Nihonfu|1=Mimana Nihon-fu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6139814}}, which had been overtaken by Silla.

== Overview ==
The title first appears in {{ill|Suiko 8|ja|推古天皇8年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suiko 8|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (600 AD). It was established during an expedition to rescue Mimana, which had been annexed by Silla. [[:en:Sakaibe no Marise|Sakaibe no Marise]], a member of the Soga clan, was appointed as the commander-in-chief (Sei Shiraiki Taishōgun), and {{ill|Hozumi no Suketari|ja|穂積祖足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi no Suketari|1=Hozumi Soso|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597084}} was appointed as the deputy general (fuku-shōgun). They led an army of approximately 10,000 troops to the Korean Peninsula. Although Sakaibe no Marise held the title, he did not personally take part in the campaign. The campaign was initially successful, capturing five fortresses and forcing Silla's surrender. They further captured six additional fortresses—Tatara, Sunara, Hochikui, Wada, Arishi no Kara, and Arara—and secured a promise of tribute to Japan. However, once the Japanese forces withdrew, Silla resumed its aggression against Mimana.

In Suiko 10 (602), {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, a younger brother of [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Shōtoku Taishi]], was appointed as the expedition general (shōgun) and given command of 25,000 troops. He advanced to [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Tsukushi Province]] in April and established a camp in the Shimogōri region. However, Prince Kume fell ill and delayed the campaign. He died in February of the following year (603), leaving the mission unfulfilled.

In Suiko 11 (603), {{ill|Prince Maroko|lt=Prince Maroko|lt=Prince Tōma|ja|当麻皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tōma|qq=}}, an older half-brother of Prince Kume, was appointed as the expedition general. He departed from Naniwa on July 3 and arrived in Harima on July 6. However, the campaign was abandoned when his wife, Princess Toneri, passed away in Akashi. Prince Tōma returned to the court, and the campaign was suspended.

Another expedition occurred in Suiko 31 (623). {{ill|Nakatomi no Kuni|ja|中臣国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kuni|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣国子|1=Nakatomi Kuniko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407186}} proposed to the Emperor that Japan should retaliate against Silla for attacking Mimana and restore Mimana to Baekje. As a result, {{ill|Sakaibe no Umamaro|ja|境部雄摩侶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaibe no Umamaro|1=Sakaibe no Umamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407177}} and Nakatomi no Kuni were appointed as generals, with several other notable figures, including {{ill|Kawabe no Neju|ja|河辺禰受|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe no Neju|1=Kawabe no Nezu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407260}}, {{ill|Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|ja|物部依網乙等|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|1=Mononobe no Yosami no Oto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66365099}}, and {{ill|Hada no Hiroba|ja|波多廣庭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hada no Hiroba|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, serving as deputy generals. They led an army of tens of thousands to the Korean Peninsula, and upon hearing of the army’s size, Silla surrendered.

== Appointments ==
=== Generals ===
* [[:en:Sakaibe no Marise|Sakaibe no Marise]] (did not participate in the campaign)
* {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}} (did not complete the campaign)
* {{ill|Prince Maroko|lt=Prince Maroko|lt=Prince Tōma|ja|当麻皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tōma|qq=}} (did not complete the campaign)
* {{ill|Nakatomi no Kuni|ja|中臣国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kuni|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣国子|1=Nakatomi Kuniko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407186}}
* {{ill|Sakaibe no Umamaro|ja|境部雄摩侶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaibe no Umamaro|1=Sakaibe no Umamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407177}}

=== Deputy Generals ===
* {{ill|Hozumi no Suketari|ja|穂積祖足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi no Suketari|1=Hozumi Soso|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597084}}
* {{ill|Kawabe no Neju|ja|河辺禰受|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe no Neju|1=Kawabe no Nezu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407260}}
* {{ill|Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|ja|物部依網乙等|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|1=Mononobe no Yosami no Oto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66365099}}
* {{ill|Hada no Hiroba|ja|波多廣庭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hada no Hiroba|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Ōmi no Ashikami no Iifuta|ja|近江脚身飯蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi no Ashikami no Iifuta|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Heguri no Ushi|ja|平群宇志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heguri no Ushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Ōtomo no (unknown name)|ja|大伴某|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no (unknown name)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Ōyake no Ikusa|ja|大宅軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyake no Ikusa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

== References ==

As a result, {{ill|Sakaibe no Umamaro|ja|境部雄摩侶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaibe no Umamaro|1=Sakaibe no Umamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407177}} and Nakatomi no Kuni were appointed as generals, with several other notable figures, including {{ill|Kawabe no Neju|ja|河辺禰受|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe no Neju|1=Kawabe no Nezu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407260}}, {{ill|Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|ja|物部依網乙等|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|1=Mononobe no Yosami no Oto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66365099}}, and {{ill|Hada no Hiroba|ja|波多廣庭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hada no Hiroba|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, serving as deputy generals. They led an army of tens of thousands to the Korean Peninsula, and upon hearing of the army’s size, Silla surrendered.

== Appointments ==
=== Generals ===
* [[:en:Sakaibe no Marise|Sakaibe no Marise]] (did not participate in the campaign)
* {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}} (did not complete the campaign)
* {{ill|Prince Maroko|lt=Prince Maroko|lt=Prince Tōma|ja|当麻皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tōma|qq=}} (did not complete the campaign)
* {{ill|Nakatomi no Kuni|ja|中臣国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kuni|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣国子|1=Nakatomi Kuniko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407186}}
* {{ill|Sakaibe no Umamaro|ja|境部雄摩侶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaibe no Umamaro|1=Sakaibe no Umamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407177}}

* {{ill|Kawabe no Neju|ja|河辺禰受|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe no Neju|1=Kawabe no Nezu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407260}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:征新羅大将軍]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q20040917]]
Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Sei Shiraiki Taishōgun''' (征新羅大将軍, "General for the Conquest of Silla") was a title used in ancient Japan for generals appointed to lead military expeditions against the kingdom of [[:en:Silla|Silla]]. The role was primarily created to reclaim the {{ill|Mimana Nihonfu|ja|任那日本府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimana Nihonfu|1=Mimana Nihon-fu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6139814}}, which had been overtaken by Silla.

== Overview ==
The title first appears in {{ill|Suiko 8|ja|推古天皇8年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suiko 8|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (600 AD). It was established during an expedition to rescue Mimana, which had been annexed by Silla. [[:en:Sakaibe no Marise|Sakaibe no Marise]], a member of the Soga clan, was appointed as the commander-in-chief (Sei Shiraiki Taishōgun), and {{ill|Hozumi no Suketari|ja|穂積祖足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi no Suketari|1=Hozumi Soso|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597084}} was appointed as the deputy general (fuku-shōgun). They led an army of approximately 10,000 troops to the Korean Peninsula. Although Sakaibe no Marise held the title, he did not personally take part in the campaign. The campaign was initially successful, capturing five fortresses and forcing Silla's surrender. They further captured six additional fortresses—Tatara, Sunara, Hochikui, Wada, Arishi no Kara, and Arara—and secured a promise of tribute to Japan. However, once the Japanese forces withdrew, Silla resumed its aggression against Mimana.

In Suiko 10 (602), {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, a younger brother of [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Shōtoku Taishi]], was appointed as the expedition general (shōgun) and given command of 25,000 troops. He advanced to [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Tsukushi Province]] in April and established a camp in the Shimogōri region. However, Prince Kume fell ill and delayed the campaign. He died in February of the following year (603), leaving the mission unfulfilled.

In Suiko 11 (603), {{ill|Prince Maroko|lt=Prince Maroko|lt=Prince Tōma|ja|当麻皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tōma|qq=}}, an older half-brother of Prince Kume, was appointed as the expedition general. He departed from Naniwa on July 3 and arrived in Harima on July 6. However, the campaign was abandoned when his wife, Princess Toneri, passed away in Akashi. Prince Tōma returned to the court, and the campaign was suspended.

Another expedition occurred in Suiko 31 (623). {{ill|Nakatomi no Kuni|ja|中臣国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kuni|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣国子|1=Nakatomi Kuniko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407186}} proposed to the Emperor that Japan should retaliate against Silla for attacking Mimana and restore Mimana to Baekje. As a result, {{ill|Sakaibe no Umamaro|ja|境部雄摩侶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaibe no Umamaro|1=Sakaibe no Umamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407177}} and Nakatomi no Kuni were appointed as generals, with several other notable figures, including {{ill|Kawabe no Neju|ja|河辺禰受|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe no Neju|1=Kawabe no Nezu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407260}}, {{ill|Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|ja|物部依網乙等|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|1=Mononobe no Yosami no Oto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66365099}}, and {{ill|Hada no Hiroba|ja|波多廣庭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hada no Hiroba|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, serving as deputy generals. They led an army of tens of thousands to the Korean Peninsula, and upon hearing of the army’s size, Silla surrendered.

== Appointments ==
=== Generals ===
* [[:en:Sakaibe no Marise|Sakaibe no Marise]] (did not participate in the campaign)
* {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}} (did not complete the campaign)
* {{ill|Prince Maroko|lt=Prince Maroko|lt=Prince Tōma|ja|当麻皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tōma|qq=}} (did not complete the campaign)
* {{ill|Nakatomi no Kuni|ja|中臣国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kuni|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣国子|1=Nakatomi Kuniko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407186}}
* {{ill|Sakaibe no Umamaro|ja|境部雄摩侶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaibe no Umamaro|1=Sakaibe no Umamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407177}}

=== Deputy Generals ===
* {{ill|Hozumi no Suketari|ja|穂積祖足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi no Suketari|1=Hozumi Soso|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597084}}
* {{ill|Kawabe no Neju|ja|河辺禰受|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe no Neju|1=Kawabe no Nezu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407260}}
* {{ill|Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|ja|物部依網乙等|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|1=Mononobe no Yosami no Oto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66365099}}
* {{ill|Hada no Hiroba|ja|波多廣庭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hada no Hiroba|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Ōmi no Ashikami no Iifuta|ja|近江脚身飯蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi no Ashikami no Iifuta|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Heguri no Ushi|ja|平群宇志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heguri no Ushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Ōtomo no (unknown name)|ja|大伴某|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no (unknown name)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Ōyake no Ikusa|ja|大宅軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyake no Ikusa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

== References ==
* [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]

'''Sei Shiraiki Taishōgun''' (征新羅大将軍, "General for the Conquest of Silla") was a title used in ancient Japan for generals appointed to lead military expeditions against the kingdom of [[:en:Silla|Silla]]. The role was primarily created to reclaim the {{ill|Mimana Nihonfu|ja|任那日本府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimana Nihonfu|1=Mimana Nihon-fu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6139814}}, which had been overtaken by Silla.

== Overview ==
The title first appears in {{ill|Suiko 8|ja|推古天皇8年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suiko 8|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (600 AD). It was established during an expedition to rescue Mimana, which had been annexed by Silla. [[:en:Sakaibe no Marise|Sakaibe no Marise]], a member of the Soga clan, was appointed as the commander-in-chief (Sei Shiraiki Taishōgun), and {{ill|Hozumi no Suketari|ja|穂積祖足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi no Suketari|1=Hozumi Soso|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597084}} was appointed as the deputy general (fuku-shōgun). They led an army of approximately 10,000 troops to the Korean Peninsula. Although Sakaibe no Marise held the title, he did not personally take part in the campaign. The campaign was initially successful, capturing five fortresses and forcing Silla's surrender. They further captured six additional fortresses—Tatara, Sunara, Hochikui, Wada, Arishi no Kara, and Arara—and secured a promise of tribute to Japan. However, once the Japanese forces withdrew, Silla resumed its aggression against Mimana.

In Suiko 10 (602), {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, a younger brother of [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Shōtoku Taishi]], was appointed as the expedition general (shōgun) and given command of 25,000 troops. He advanced to [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Tsukushi Province]] in April and established a camp in the Shimogōri region. However, Prince Kume fell ill and delayed the campaign. He died in February of the following year (603), leaving the mission unfulfilled.

In Suiko 11 (603), {{ill|Prince Maroko|lt=Prince Maroko|lt=Prince Tōma|ja|当麻皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tōma|qq=}}, an older half-brother of Prince Kume, was appointed as the expedition general. He departed from Naniwa on July 3 and arrived in Harima on July 6. However, the campaign was abandoned when his wife, Princess Toneri, passed away in Akashi. Prince Tōma returned to the court, and the campaign was suspended.

Another expedition occurred in Suiko 31 (623). {{ill|Nakatomi no Kuni|ja|中臣国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kuni|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣国子|1=Nakatomi Kuniko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407186}} proposed to the Emperor that Japan should retaliate against Silla for attacking Mimana and restore Mimana to Baekje. As a result, {{ill|Sakaibe no Umamaro|ja|境部雄摩侶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaibe no Umamaro|1=Sakaibe no Umamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407177}} and Nakatomi no Kuni were appointed as generals, with several other notable figures, including {{ill|Kawabe no Neju|ja|河辺禰受|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe no Neju|1=Kawabe no Nezu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407260}}, {{ill|Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|ja|物部依網乙等|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|1=Mononobe no Yosami no Oto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66365099}}, and {{ill|Hada no Hiroba|ja|波多廣庭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hada no Hiroba|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, serving as deputy generals. They led an army of tens of thousands to the Korean Peninsula, and upon hearing of the army’s size, Silla surrendered.

== Appointments ==
=== Generals ===
* [[:en:Sakaibe no Marise|Sakaibe no Marise]] (did not participate in the campaign)
* {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}} (did not complete the campaign)
* {{ill|Prince Maroko|lt=Prince Maroko|lt=Prince Tōma|ja|当麻皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tōma|qq=}} (did not complete the campaign)
* {{ill|Nakatomi no Kuni|ja|中臣国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kuni|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣国子|1=Nakatomi Kuniko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407186}}
* {{ill|Sakaibe no Umamaro|ja|境部雄摩侶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaibe no Umamaro|1=Sakaibe no Umamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407177}}

=== Deputy Generals ===
* {{ill|Hozumi no Suketari|ja|穂積祖足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi no Suketari|1=Hozumi Soso|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597084}}
* {{ill|Kawabe no Neju|ja|河辺禰受|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe no Neju|1=Kawabe no Nezu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407260}}
* {{ill|Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|ja|物部依網乙等|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|1=Mononobe no Yosami no Oto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66365099}}
* {{ill|Hada no Hiroba|ja|波多廣庭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hada no Hiroba|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Ōmi no Ashikami no Iifuta|ja|近江脚身飯蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi no Ashikami no Iifuta|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Heguri no Ushi|ja|平群宇志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heguri no Ushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Ōtomo no (unknown name)|ja|大伴某|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no (unknown name)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Ōyake no Ikusa|ja|大宅軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyake no Ikusa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:征新羅大将軍]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:征新羅大将軍]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:征新羅大将軍]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20040917]]
Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Sei Shiraiki Taishōgun''' (征新羅大将軍, "General for the Conquest of Silla") was a title used in ancient Japan for generals appointed to lead military expeditions against the kingdom of [[:en:Silla|Silla]]. The role was primarily created to reclaim the {{ill|Mimana Nihonfu|ja|任那日本府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimana Nihonfu|1=Mimana Nihon-fu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6139814}}, which had been overtaken by Silla.

== Overview ==
The title first appears in {{ill|Suiko 8|ja|推古天皇8年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suiko 8|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (600 AD). It was established during an expedition to rescue Mimana, which had been annexed by Silla. [[:en:Sakaibe no Marise|Sakaibe no Marise]], a member of the Soga clan, was appointed as the commander-in-chief (Sei Shiraiki Taishōgun), and {{ill|Hozumi no Suketari|ja|穂積祖足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi no Suketari|1=Hozumi Soso|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597084}} was appointed as the deputy general (fuku-shōgun). They led an army of approximately 10,000 troops to the Korean Peninsula. Although Sakaibe no Marise held the title, he did not personally take part in the campaign. The campaign was initially successful, capturing five fortresses and forcing Silla's surrender. They further captured six additional fortresses—Tatara, Sunara, Hochikui, Wada, Arishi no Kara, and Arara—and secured a promise of tribute to Japan. However, once the Japanese forces withdrew, Silla resumed its aggression against Mimana.

In Suiko 10 (602), {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, a younger brother of [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Shōtoku Taishi]], was appointed as the expedition general (shōgun) and given command of 25,000 troops. He advanced to [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Tsukushi Province]] in April and established a camp in the Shimogōri region. However, Prince Kume fell ill and delayed the campaign. He died in February of the following year (603), leaving the mission unfulfilled.

In Suiko 11 (603), {{ill|Prince Maroko|lt=Prince Maroko|lt=Prince Tōma|ja|当麻皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tōma|qq=}}, an older half-brother of Prince Kume, was appointed as the expedition general. He departed from Naniwa on July 3 and arrived in Harima on July 6. However, the campaign was abandoned when his wife, Princess Toneri, passed away in Akashi. Prince Tōma returned to the court, and the campaign was suspended.

Another expedition occurred in Suiko 31 (623). {{ill|Nakatomi no Kuni|ja|中臣国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kuni|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣国子|1=Nakatomi Kuniko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407186}} proposed to the Emperor that Japan should retaliate against Silla for attacking Mimana and restore Mimana to Baekje. As a result, {{ill|Sakaibe no Umamaro|ja|境部雄摩侶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaibe no Umamaro|1=Sakaibe no Umamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407177}} and Nakatomi no Kuni were appointed as generals, with several other notable figures, including {{ill|Kawabe no Neju|ja|河辺禰受|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe no Neju|1=Kawabe no Nezu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407260}}, {{ill|Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|ja|物部依網乙等|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|1=Mononobe no Yosami no Oto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66365099}}, and {{ill|Hada no Hiroba|ja|波多廣庭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hada no Hiroba|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, serving as deputy generals. They led an army of tens of thousands to the Korean Peninsula, and upon hearing of the army’s size, Silla surrendered.

== Appointments ==
=== Generals ===
* [[:en:Sakaibe no Marise|Sakaibe no Marise]] (did not participate in the campaign)
* {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}} (did not complete the campaign)
* {{ill|Prince Maroko|lt=Prince Maroko|lt=Prince Tōma|ja|当麻皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tōma|qq=}} (did not complete the campaign)
* {{ill|Nakatomi no Kuni|ja|中臣国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kuni|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣国子|1=Nakatomi Kuniko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407186}}
* {{ill|Sakaibe no Umamaro|ja|境部雄摩侶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaibe no Umamaro|1=Sakaibe no Umamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407177}}

=== Deputy Generals ===
* {{ill|Hozumi no Suketari|ja|穂積祖足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi no Suketari|1=Hozumi Soso|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597084}}
* {{ill|Kawabe no Neju|ja|河辺禰受|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe no Neju|1=Kawabe no Nezu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407260}}
* {{ill|Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|ja|物部依網乙等|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|1=Mononobe no Yosami no Oto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66365099}}
* {{ill|Hada no Hiroba|ja|波多廣庭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hada no Hiroba|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Ōmi no Ashikami no Iifuta|ja|近江脚身飯蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi no Ashikami no Iifuta|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Heguri no Ushi|ja|平群宇志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heguri no Ushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Ōtomo no (unknown name)|ja|大伴某|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no (unknown name)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Ōyake no Ikusa|ja|大宅軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyake no Ikusa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

== References ==
* [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Plans for the Conquest of Silla|ja|新羅征討計画|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Plans for the Conquest of Silla|1=Fujiwara no Nakamaro's planned invasion of Silla|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10307032}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:征新羅大将軍]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:征新羅大将軍]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:征新羅大将軍]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20040917]]
Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Sei Shiraiki Taishōgun''' (征新羅大将軍, "General for the Conquest of Silla") was a title used in ancient Japan for generals appointed to lead military expeditions against the kingdom of {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla}}. The role was primarily created to reclaim the {{ill|Mimana Nihonfu|ja|任那日本府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimana Nihonfu|1=Mimana Nihon-fu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6139814}}, which had been overtaken by Silla.

== Overview ==
The title first appears in {{ill|Suiko 8|ja|推古天皇8年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suiko 8|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (600 AD). It was established during an expedition to rescue Mimana, which had been annexed by Silla. {{ill|Sakaibe no Marise|en|Sakaibe no Marise|WD=Q7402774|lt=Sakaibe no Marise}}, a member of the Soga clan, was appointed as the commander-in-chief (Sei Shiraiki Taishōgun), and {{ill|Hozumi no Suketari|ja|穂積祖足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi no Suketari|1=Hozumi Soso|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597084}} was appointed as the deputy general (fuku-shōgun). They led an army of approximately 10,000 troops to the Korean Peninsula. Although Sakaibe no Marise held the title, he did not personally take part in the campaign. The campaign was initially successful, capturing five fortresses and forcing Silla's surrender. They further captured six additional fortresses—Tatara, Sunara, Hochikui, Wada, Arishi no Kara, and Arara—and secured a promise of tribute to Japan. However, once the Japanese forces withdrew, Silla resumed its aggression against Mimana.

In Suiko 10 (602), {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, a younger brother of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|WD=Q263972|lt=Shōtoku Taishi}}, was appointed as the expedition general (shōgun) and given command of 25,000 troops. He advanced to {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en|Tsukushi Province|WD=Q1206461|lt=Tsukushi Province}} in April and established a camp in the Shimogōri region. However, Prince Kume fell ill and delayed the campaign. He died in February of the following year (603), leaving the mission unfulfilled.

In Suiko 11 (603), {{ill|Prince Maroko|lt=Prince Maroko|lt=Prince Tōma|ja|当麻皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tōma|qq=}}, an older half-brother of Prince Kume, was appointed as the expedition general. He departed from Naniwa on July 3 and arrived in Harima on July 6. However, the campaign was abandoned when his wife, Princess Toneri, passed away in Akashi. Prince Tōma returned to the court, and the campaign was suspended.

Another expedition occurred in Suiko 31 (623). {{ill|Nakatomi no Kuni|ja|中臣国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kuni|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣国子|1=Nakatomi Kuniko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407186}} proposed to the Emperor that Japan should retaliate against Silla for attacking Mimana and restore Mimana to Baekje. As a result, {{ill|Sakaibe no Umamaro|ja|境部雄摩侶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaibe no Umamaro|1=Sakaibe no Umamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407177}} and Nakatomi no Kuni were appointed as generals, with several other notable figures, including {{ill|Kawabe no Neju|ja|河辺禰受|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe no Neju|1=Kawabe no Nezu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407260}}, {{ill|Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|ja|物部依網乙等|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|1=Mononobe no Yosami no Oto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66365099}}, and {{ill|Hada no Hiroba|ja|波多廣庭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hada no Hiroba|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, serving as deputy generals. They led an army of tens of thousands to the Korean Peninsula, and upon hearing of the army’s size, Silla surrendered.

== Appointments ==
=== Generals ===
* {{ill|Sakaibe no Marise|en|Sakaibe no Marise|WD=Q7402774|lt=Sakaibe no Marise}} (did not participate in the campaign)
* {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}} (did not complete the campaign)
* {{ill|Prince Maroko|lt=Prince Maroko|lt=Prince Tōma|ja|当麻皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tōma|qq=}} (did not complete the campaign)
* {{ill|Nakatomi no Kuni|ja|中臣国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kuni|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣国子|1=Nakatomi Kuniko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407186}}
* {{ill|Sakaibe no Umamaro|ja|境部雄摩侶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaibe no Umamaro|1=Sakaibe no Umamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407177}}

=== Deputy Generals ===
* {{ill|Hozumi no Suketari|ja|穂積祖足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi no Suketari|1=Hozumi Soso|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597084}}
* {{ill|Kawabe no Neju|ja|河辺禰受|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe no Neju|1=Kawabe no Nezu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407260}}
* {{ill|Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|ja|物部依網乙等|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|1=Mononobe no Yosami no Oto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66365099}}
* {{ill|Hada no Hiroba|ja|波多廣庭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hada no Hiroba|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Ōmi no Ashikami no Iifuta|ja|近江脚身飯蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi no Ashikami no Iifuta|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Heguri no Ushi|ja|平群宇志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heguri no Ushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Ōtomo no (unknown name)|ja|大伴某|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no (unknown name)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Ōyake no Ikusa|ja|大宅軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyake no Ikusa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

== References ==
* {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Plans for the Conquest of Silla|ja|新羅征討計画|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Plans for the Conquest of Silla|1=Fujiwara no Nakamaro's planned invasion of Silla|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10307032}}

Another expedition occurred in Suiko 31 (623). {{ill|Nakatomi no Kuni|ja|中臣国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kuni|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣国子|1=Nakatomi Kuniko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407186}} proposed to the Emperor that Japan should retaliate against Silla for attacking Mimana and restore Mimana to Baekje. As a result, {{ill|Sakaibe no Umamaro|ja|境部雄摩侶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaibe no Umamaro|1=Sakaibe no Umamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407177}} and Nakatomi no Kuni were appointed as generals, with several other notable figures, including {{ill|Kawabe no Neju|ja|河辺禰受|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe no Neju|1=Kawabe no Nezu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407260}}, {{ill|Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|ja|物部依網乙等|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|1=Mononobe no Yosami no Oto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66365099}}, and {{ill|Hada no Hiroba|ja|波多廣庭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hada no Hiroba|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, serving as deputy generals. They led an army of tens of thousands to the Korean Peninsula, and upon hearing of the army’s size, Silla surrendered.

== Appointments ==
=== Generals ===
* {{ill|Sakaibe no Marise|en|Sakaibe no Marise|WD=Q7402774|lt=Sakaibe no Marise}} (did not participate in the campaign)
* {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}} (did not complete the campaign)
* {{ill|Prince Maroko|lt=Prince Maroko|lt=Prince Tōma|ja|当麻皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tōma|qq=}} (did not complete the campaign)
* {{ill|Nakatomi no Kuni|ja|中臣国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kuni|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣国子|1=Nakatomi Kuniko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407186}}
* {{ill|Sakaibe no Umamaro|ja|境部雄摩侶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaibe no Umamaro|1=Sakaibe no Umamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407177}}

=== Deputy Generals ===
