<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:征新羅大将軍]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:征新羅大将軍]]

'''Sei Shiraiki Taishōgun''' (征新羅大将軍, "General for the Conquest of Silla") was a title used in ancient Japan for generals appointed to lead military expeditions against the kingdom of {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla}}. The role was primarily created to reclaim the {{ill|Mimana Nihonfu|ja|任那日本府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimana Nihonfu|1=Mimana Nihon-fu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6139814}}, which had been overtaken by Silla.

== Overview ==
The title first appears in {{ill|Suiko 8|ja|推古天皇8年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suiko 8|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (600 AD). It was established during an expedition to rescue Mimana, which had been annexed by Silla. {{ill|Sakaibe no Marise|en|Sakaibe no Marise|WD=Q7402774|lt=Sakaibe no Marise}}, a member of the Soga clan, was appointed as the commander-in-chief (Sei Shiraiki Taishōgun), and {{ill|Hozumi no Suketari|ja|穂積祖足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi no Suketari|1=Hozumi Soso|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597084}} was appointed as the deputy general (fuku-shōgun). They led an army of approximately 10,000 troops to the Korean Peninsula. Although Sakaibe no Marise held the title, he did not personally take part in the campaign. The campaign was initially successful, capturing five fortresses and forcing Silla's surrender. They further captured six additional fortresses—Tatara, Sunara, Hochikui, Wada, Arishi no Kara, and Arara—and secured a promise of tribute to Japan. However, once the Japanese forces withdrew, Silla resumed its aggression against Mimana.

In Suiko 10 (602), {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, a younger brother of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|WD=Q263972|lt=Shōtoku Taishi}}, was appointed as the expedition general (shōgun) and given command of 25,000 troops. He advanced to {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en|Tsukushi Province|WD=Q1206461|lt=Tsukushi Province}} in April and established a camp in the Shimogōri region. However, Prince Kume fell ill and delayed the campaign. He died in February of the following year (603), leaving the mission unfulfilled.

In Suiko 11 (603), {{ill|Prince Maroko|lt=Prince Maroko|lt=Prince Tōma|ja|当麻皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tōma|qq=}}, an older half-brother of Prince Kume, was appointed as the expedition general. He departed from Naniwa on July 3 and arrived in Harima on July 6. However, the campaign was abandoned when his wife, Princess Toneri, passed away in Akashi. Prince Tōma returned to the court, and the campaign was suspended.

Another expedition occurred in Suiko 31 (623). {{ill|Nakatomi no Kuni|ja|中臣国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kuni|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣国子|1=Nakatomi Kuniko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407186}} proposed to the Emperor that Japan should retaliate against Silla for attacking Mimana and restore Mimana to Baekje. As a result, {{ill|Sakaibe no Umamaro|ja|境部雄摩侶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaibe no Umamaro|1=Sakaibe no Umamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407177}} and Nakatomi no Kuni were appointed as generals, with several other notable figures, including {{ill|Kawabe no Neju|ja|河辺禰受|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe no Neju|1=Kawabe no Nezu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407260}}, {{ill|Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|ja|物部依網乙等|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|1=Mononobe no Yosami no Oto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66365099}}, and {{ill|Hada no Hiroba|ja|波多廣庭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hada no Hiroba|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, serving as deputy generals. They led an army of tens of thousands to the Korean Peninsula, and upon hearing of the army’s size, Silla surrendered.

== Appointments ==
=== Generals ===
* {{ill|Sakaibe no Marise|en|Sakaibe no Marise|WD=Q7402774|lt=Sakaibe no Marise}} (did not participate in the campaign)
* {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}} (did not complete the campaign)
* {{ill|Prince Maroko|lt=Prince Maroko|lt=Prince Tōma|ja|当麻皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tōma|qq=}} (did not complete the campaign)
* {{ill|Nakatomi no Kuni|ja|中臣国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kuni|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣国子|1=Nakatomi Kuniko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407186}}
* {{ill|Sakaibe no Umamaro|ja|境部雄摩侶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaibe no Umamaro|1=Sakaibe no Umamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407177}}

=== Deputy Generals ===
* {{ill|Hozumi no Suketari|ja|穂積祖足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi no Suketari|1=Hozumi Soso|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597084}}
* {{ill|Kawabe no Neju|ja|河辺禰受|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe no Neju|1=Kawabe no Nezu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407260}}
* {{ill|Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|ja|物部依網乙等|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|1=Mononobe no Yosami no Oto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66365099}}
* {{ill|Hada no Hiroba|ja|波多廣庭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hada no Hiroba|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Ōmi no Ashikami no Iifuta|ja|近江脚身飯蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi no Ashikami no Iifuta|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Heguri no Ushi|ja|平群宇志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heguri no Ushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Ōtomo no (unknown name)|ja|大伴某|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no (unknown name)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Ōyake no Ikusa|ja|大宅軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyake no Ikusa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

The role was primarily created to reclaim the {{ill|Mimana Nihonfu|ja|任那日本府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimana Nihonfu|1=Mimana Nihon-fu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6139814}}, which had been overtaken by Silla.

== Overview ==
The title first appears in {{ill|Suiko 8|ja|推古天皇8年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suiko 8|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (600 AD). It was established during an expedition to rescue Mimana, which had been annexed by Silla. {{ill|Sakaibe no Marise|en|Sakaibe no Marise|WD=Q7402774|lt=Sakaibe no Marise|1=Sakaibe no Marise}}, a member of the Soga clan, was appointed as the commander-in-chief (Sei Shiraiki Taishōgun), and {{ill|Hozumi no Suketari|ja|穂積祖足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi no Suketari|1=Hozumi Soso|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597084}} was appointed as the deputy general (fuku-shōgun). They led an army of approximately 10,000 troops to the Korean Peninsula. Although Sakaibe no Marise held the title, he did not personally take part in the campaign. The campaign was initially successful, capturing five fortresses and forcing Silla's surrender. They further captured six additional fortresses—Tatara, Sunara, Hochikui, Wada, Arishi no Kara, and Arara—and secured a promise of tribute to Japan. However, once the Japanese forces withdrew, Silla resumed its aggression against Mimana.

In Suiko 10 (602), {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, a younger brother of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|WD=Q263972|lt=Shōtoku Taishi|1=Prince Shōtoku}}, was appointed as the expedition general (shōgun) and given command of 25,000 troops. He advanced to {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en|Tsukushi Province|WD=Q1206461|lt=Tsukushi Province|1=Tsukushi Province}} in April and established a camp in the Shimogōri region. However, Prince Kume fell ill and delayed the campaign. He died in February of the following year (603), leaving the mission unfulfilled.

In Suiko 11 (603), {{ill|Prince Maroko|lt=Prince Maroko|lt=Prince Tōma|ja|当麻皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tōma|qq=}}, an older half-brother of Prince Kume, was appointed as the expedition general. He departed from Naniwa on July 3 and arrived in Harima on July 6. However, the campaign was abandoned when his wife, Princess Toneri, passed away in Akashi. Prince Tōma returned to the court, and the campaign was suspended.

Another expedition occurred in Suiko 31 (623). {{ill|Nakatomi no Kuni|ja|中臣国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kuni|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣国子|1=Nakatomi Kuniko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407186}} proposed to the Emperor that Japan should retaliate against Silla for attacking Mimana and restore Mimana to Baekje. As a result, {{ill|Sakaibe no Umamaro|ja|境部雄摩侶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaibe no Umamaro|1=Q55407177|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407177}} and Nakatomi no Kuni were appointed as generals, with several other notable figures, including {{ill|Kawabe no Neju|ja|河辺禰受|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe no Neju|1=Kawabe no Nezu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407260}}, {{ill|Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|ja|物部依網乙等|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|1=Mononobe no Yosami no Oto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66365099}}, and {{ill|Hada no Hiroba|ja|波多廣庭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hada no Hiroba|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, serving as deputy generals. They led an army of tens of thousands to the Korean Peninsula, and upon hearing of the army’s size, Silla surrendered.

== Appointments ==
=== Generals ===
* {{ill|Sakaibe no Marise|en|Sakaibe no Marise|WD=Q7402774|lt=Sakaibe no Marise|1=Sakaibe no Marise}} (did not participate in the campaign)
* {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}} (did not complete the campaign)
* {{ill|Prince Maroko|lt=Prince Maroko|lt=Prince Tōma|ja|当麻皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tōma|qq=}} (did not complete the campaign)
* {{ill|Nakatomi no Kuni|ja|中臣国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Kuni|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣国子|1=Nakatomi Kuniko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407186}}
* {{ill|Sakaibe no Umamaro|ja|境部雄摩侶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaibe no Umamaro|1=Q55407177|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407177}}

=== Deputy Generals ===
* {{ill|Hozumi no Suketari|ja|穂積祖足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi no Suketari|1=Hozumi Soso|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597084}}
* {{ill|Kawabe no Neju|ja|河辺禰受|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe no Neju|1=Kawabe no Nezu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407260}}
* {{ill|Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|ja|物部依網乙等|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Iyami no Ototo|1=Mononobe no Yosami no Oto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q66365099}}
* {{ill|Hada no Hiroba|ja|波多廣庭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hada no Hiroba|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Ōmi no Ashikami no Iifuta|ja|近江脚身飯蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi no Ashikami no Iifuta|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Heguri no Ushi|ja|平群宇志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heguri no Ushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Ōtomo no (unknown name)|ja|大伴某|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no (unknown name)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Ōyake no Ikusa|ja|大宅軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyake no Ikusa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
